prompt:
You are shown one page from a SEC 10-K filing rendered as an image.
Item "5. Market for Registrant's Common Equity, Related Stockholder Matters and Issuer Purchases of Equity Securities" ENDS on this page; the next section is "Item 6. Reserved".
First locate where Item 5 stops on this page:
- If the "Item 6" heading is visible on this page, Item 5 ends on the line directly ABOVE that heading.
- Ignore ALL text below that heading; it belongs to the next section, not Item 5.
- The target section may have started on a previous page and may leave only a short carryover at the very TOP of this page.
- If the next-section heading appears near the top, the answer may be only the few lines immediately above that heading.
- Only if no later section heading appears on this page should you use the bottom of the page as Item 5's ending.
Then transcribe VERBATIM the LAST 5 lines of Item 5 right before the section ends. The answer must come only from Item 5, even if the next section occupies most of the page. Ignore running headers, page numbers, footers.
Output ONLY the transcribed text, no commentary, no quotes, no formatting.
text:
Notwithstanding any statement to the contrary in any of our previous or future Filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission, the
following information relating to the price performance of our common stock shall not be deemed "filed" with the Commission or "soliciting
material" under the Exchange Act and shall not be incorporated by reference into any such filings.

The following graph compares, for the five year period ended December 31, 2025, the total cumulative stockholder return on Company's
common stock with that of the S&P 500 Index, the NASDAQ Composite Index, the NYSE Composite Index and the RDG Internet Composite Index.
Measurement points are the last trading day of each of the Company's fiscal years ended December 31, 2020, December 31, 2021, December 31,
2022, December 31, 2023, December 31, 2024 and December 31, 2025. Total cumulative stockholder return assumes $100 invested at the beginning of
the period and that all dividends are reinvested on the date of payment. The S&P 500, NASDAQ Composite Index, the NYSE Composite Index, the
stocks represented in the RDG Internet Composite Index, respectively, and reinvestment of any dividends. Historical stock price
performance should not be relied upon as an indication of future stock price performance.
